content.introduction: Bubble sort repeatedly swaps adjacent out-of-order elements, pushing larger elements toward the end each pass.

sections.title: 7.2.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define adjacent comparison and pass invariant where the largest unsorted element bubbles to final position.
examples: Explain pass invariant after each outer loop
Show stable behavior under equal keys
Compare bubble and insertion movement patterns

sections.title: 7.2.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe nested-loop workflow, swap conditions, and early-stop optimization with swap flags.
examples: Use no-swap flag for early termination
Limit inner loop by sorted tail boundary

sections.title: 7.2.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Complexity is O(n^2) worst/average, O(n) best with early-stop, and O(1) extra space.
examples: Evaluate bubble sort for teaching vs production
Compare swap count on random inputs
Use only for tiny or demonstrative datasets

keyTakeaways: Use bubble sort mainly for learning scenarios
